entities.name: PATTERN ANALYSIS
entities.description: A technique consisting of joint and complementary use of classification and ordination techniques to identify groupings of genotypes and environments

entities.name: CLASSIFICATION
entities.description: A technique used within pattern analysis to group data

entities.name: ORDINATION TECHNIQUES
entities.description: Techniques used within pattern analysis to represent data in a lower-dimensional space

entities.name: GRAIN-YIELD
entities.description: The agricultural productivity measure used as the primary data for analysis

entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The crop species used in the multi-environment trials

entities.name: GENOTYPES
entities.description: The 12 different genetic variations of sorghum evaluated in the study

entities.name: ENVIRONMENTS
entities.description: The 25 different locations and conditions where the sorghum genotypes were grown

entities.name: GEOGRAPHICAL ORIGIN
entities.description: The diverse source locations from which the sorghum genotypes were obtained

entities.name: GENETIC DIVERSITY
entities.description: The variation in genetic characteristics among the selected sorghum genotypes

entities.name: PHOTOPERIOD SENSITIVITY
entities.description: The response of sorghum genotypes to the length of the day

entities.name: GENOTYPE X ENVIRONMENT INTERACTION
entities.description: The interaction between the genetic makeup of a crop and the environment it is grown in

entities.name: MEGA-ENVIRONMENTS
entities.description: Large-scale environmental groupings that share similar characteristics

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: The geographical region representing one of the identified mega-environments

entities.name: ASIA
entities.description: The geographical region representing one of the identified mega-environments

entities.name: GENOTYPE ADAPTATION PATTERNS
entities.description: The specific ways in which different genotypes adapt to different mega-environments
